participants.som: Product Marketing lead — analyst relations, thought leadership (Speaker 5)
participants.bodhi: Brand lead (absent) — content design, field marketing, Reddit

persona_influence.source_quote: The personas that we target generally split between procurement and like in-house legal teams. So, the way I think about the split is sixty thirty or fifty five, thirty five on procurement to legal. And then finance and IT and sales are, you know, heavy influencers in the ten percent range. And folks that have digital transformation is also another big one.
persona_influence.speaker: Ron (Demand & Growth lead)

digital_transformation.note: Called out as 'another big one' but unquantified

cpo.rationale: Top of procurement group, primary buyer persona at Sirion

gc.rationale: Senior legal, but legal is secondary per Ron's 60/30 split

vplo.rationale: CLM evaluator and champion within legal, under GC authority

pd.rationale: Operational procurement under CPO, project lead for CLM on procurement side

cfo.rationale: Finance influencer, part of ~10% combined group

cio.rationale: IT influencer, veto power on SaaS but not primary driver

cto.rationale: Technical gatekeeper, under CIO in CLM buying

cm.rationale: Operational user, end-user champion or blocker during POC

sales_cycle.note: 3 quarters average, some shorter some longer depending on deal size
sales_cycle.speaker: Ron

target_segment.source_quote: these questions can be applied to enterprise personas but more heavily indexed on SMB personas. And SMBs are not somebody we target.
target_segment.speaker: Som (Product Marketing)
target_segment.note_from_dj: Enterprise buyers may NOT qualify searches with 'enterprise' — they may just ask 'which is the best CLM software' without the qualifier

sirion_positioning.current_strategy: Moved narrative away from post-signature ~3 years ago, highlighting pre-sign and full-stack. Recently brought post-signature back slightly.
sirion_positioning.dj_quote: we changed our narrative at least three years ago, where we moved away from post signature, and we started highlighting everything else as well. It's only recently that somebody said okay post signature is such a big strength for us. So we should not completely move away from it.

clm_lifecycle.post_signature: Sirion WINS head-to-head, 18/85 URLs reinforce this
clm_lifecycle.pre_signature: Content exists but not dominating AI citations

partnerships.ibm: Strategic partnership, both product level and GTM level
partnerships.oracle: Stronger partnership than iCertis has with Oracle

icertis.strengths: Full-stack CLM perception, backlinks from Microsoft/IBM/Oracle/Salesforce
icertis.vulnerability: Content quality is LOWER than Sirion. Authority comes from enterprise tech backlinks not content.
icertis.awareness_stage_gap: No backlinks or authority found at awareness stage

marketing_structure.brand: Bodhi leads — content design, field marketing, Reddit
marketing_structure.product_marketing: Som leads — analyst relations, thought leadership

ai_citation_insights.product_comparisons: Disproportionately cited by AI even from low-DA sites
ai_citation_insights.industry_specific_da: Specific industry domain authority matters more than raw DA score
ai_citation_insights.nested_questions: Sequential user questions change what AI cites — Som confirmed this behavior

tools_in_use.name: Profound
tools_in_use.purpose: AI question tracking, generates hundreds of questions from keywords, tracks ranking

tools_in_use.name: UserGems
tools_in_use.purpose: Analyzes online info, generates pitches for SDR team

content_strategy.content_gap: NOT content quality — Sirion has the highest content score. THE gap is authority/backlinks from high-DA industry-relevant domains.

analyst_data.forrester_wave: Sirion received highest scores in: vision, roadmap, contract review, negotiation execution, contract analysis, obligation management, contract governance
analyst_data.crm_ratings: Ranked highest in all four use cases: pre-signature, post-signature 4.22, full lifecycle 4.20

buyer_pain_points: How to reduce contract cycle time in enterprise
CLM platforms with fastest implementation time for enterprise
Contract management AI that legal and procurement teams can actually both use

minimum_question_count.note: DJ and Som pushed back on 10-question sample — 'on the basis of ten questions, can we make this conclusion?' — need 100+ for credibility

clm_advisor_recommendation_rate.dj_feedback: Prefers to stop at feature/capability recommendations to maintain credibility, not explicit vendor recommendations
